initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> Player RED has countered with 47 ZUP. This is still well below my maximum willingness to pay of 60 ZUP. I have 1 proposal left before I must accept or reject. I should try to lower the price one more time to get closer to my ideal outcome, perhaps meeting in the middle of their last offer (47) and my previous offer (45), or just slightly lower. Let's try 46 ZUP. If they reject this, I will likely have to accept 47 in the next turn since it is still a good deal for me. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I hear your point about overheads. 47 is better than 50, but I really want to close this deal at 46 ZUP. It's a fair middle ground between our last two offers and still gives you a solid 6 ZUP profit over your cost. If you agree, we can finalize this immediately. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. The response structure follows the required order and tags correctly.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Proposing 46 ZUP is better than 47 ZUP for the player (saving 1 ZUP). Since the max willingness is 60, any price between 40 and 60 is positive utility. 46 is a logical attempt to squeeze the last bit of value before the limit of 3 proposals is reached.
3. Opponent plausibility: OK. The opponent moved from 50 to 47 (a drop of 3). A counter to 46 (a drop of 1) is a reasonable concession. The opponent has shown flexibility. If 46 is rejected, the opponent is likely to hold firm at 47 or ask for 47.5 (which must be rounded to 47 or 48). Since I only have 1 proposal left, if they reject 46, I will be forced to accept 47 or reject. 46 is a safe "last try" to see if they can go lower.
4. Consistency: OK. The reasoning explicitly states this is the second proposal and the strategy to try 46. The message matches the proposal.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is the 2nd proposal. The limit is 3. The format is correct.
</critique>
